title: paeonia foemina var. humilis
intro: Paeonia foemina var. humilis, also known as low-growing woodland peony, is a variety of flowering plant in the genus Paeonia. It is native to North America and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving menstrual cramps, and soothing digestive issues. Paeonia foemina var. humilis can be prepared as a tincture, tea, or salve for its medicinal purposes, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for therapeutic purposes.
